symptoms.symptom_name: Profound pallor with early mottling of the foot/lower leg

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Caregiver-observable proxy for critically reduced arterial perfusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive anaesthesia and motor paralysis (foot drop)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Complete sensory and motor loss indicates advanced nerve/muscle ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known peripheral arterial disease with claudication, on clopidogrel/atorvastatin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pre-existing atherosclerotic disease provides a substrate for acute in-situ thrombosis.
Suggests advancing muscle ischaemia, a marker of more advanced tissue compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent popliteal, dorsalis pedis, and posterior tibial pulses with weak palpable femoral pulse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the level of complete arterial occlusion; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt cut-off in the superficial femoral or popliteal artery on CT angiography

investigation_details.expected_result: Abrupt cut-off in the superficial femoral or popliteal artery
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Level of occlusion unconfirmed; telehealth cannot obtain this and should recommend it occur as part of emergency vascular assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms level and extent of occlusion, guiding emergency revascularisation planning.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Pallor, progressive anaesthesia/paralysis, known PAD, and a firm tender calf together reproduce the classic 6 Ps constellation with features suggesting advanced ischaemia, mandating emergency ambulance dispatch to a vascular surgery centre.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the level and extent of arterial occlusion to guide emergency revascularisation; cannot be performed via telehealth.
Screens for rhabdomyolysis/reperfusion injury risk given the firm tender calf suggesting significant muscle ischaemia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient vascular studies as first step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would dangerously delay time-critical revascularisation within the narrow limb-salvage window.
